changes_summary: Revised the plan to delete `usePanes()` entirely, use a complete `createContext` inventory regex, audit recomposed wrapper hooks alongside provider declarations, define one concrete fat-context counting rule, explicitly exclude local UI token contexts from migration scope, and use grep-driven checklists for all panes callers, tests, and provider-tree touch points.

success_criteria.criterion: `src/shared/state/panesStore.ts` exists and exports selector-based panes state APIs used by production consumers.
No production file imports `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()` after the migration.
All former `usePanes()` call sites are migrated to dedicated selector hooks or explicit narrow grouped selectors, with no broad panes compatibility facade left behind.

`docs/state_management_context_audit.md` is committed and covers both remaining `createContext` provider declarations and exported recomposed wrapper hooks, including field count, consumer count, decision, and rationale.
The audit inventory method captures generic and namespaced context declarations in this repo rather than relying on the incomplete literal `createContext\(` grep.
Any additional context surface that exceeds both audit thresholds is migrated or narrowed within the same milestone branch.
A fat-context guard exists, uses the same documented counting rule as the audit, and is wired into an automated repo check.
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes cleanly after the migration and any required test updates.
`npm test` passes at baseline after the panes migration and any additional required audit-driven migrations.
Selector hooks are predominantly single-purpose, and grouped selectors are only used where a caller truly needs multiple related pane fields or actions.
The audit keeps scope tight by documenting framework/local UI token contexts as out-of-scope instead of migrating them unnecessarily.
Opening one pane does not visually re-render unrelated panes when checked with the telemetry overlay in a running app.
Mobile exclusive-pane behavior remains unchanged on a small viewport: opening pane B closes pane A as before.

assumptions: The milestone remains centered on panes; any additional context migration is only done when the audited surface actually exceeds both thresholds and is required to satisfy the brief.
A panes bootstrap component is acceptable as long as `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, and `usePanes()` are removed and downstream consumers read from selector-based state instead of context.
The repo-level fat-context guard can share logic with the audit report so the documented field counts and automated enforcement stay consistent.
Local UI token contexts under shared UI primitives are documented in the audit but treated as out-of-scope unless they are clearly being used as shared mutable application state.
